summary: Carbondale's Town Center Property was previously partnered with Artspace Projects, Inc. (Colorado's Space to Create program) for a 9% LIHTC application that CHFA denied in May 2024 Round One. After Artspace's contract concluded in late 2025, the Town engaged Robert Schultz Consulting to draft a new master-developer RFP. The Town's housing program is funded by a voter-approved 6% short-term-rental excise tax (Ballot 2A, November 2022), and the deed-restricted Community Housing Program is administered under contract by the Garfield County Housing Authority. This brief is a post-audit surgical strip — only claims with verbatim source quotes remain. A fuller source-first rewrite is in progress.

paragraphs.text: The Town selected Artspace Projects, Inc. as the original master developer for the Town Center site, with Artspace operating in Colorado through the state's Space to Create program — Carbondale is the fourth Space to Create community.

CHFA's May 30, 2024 Round One award announcement lists 13 awarded projects across Colorado; the Carbondale Town Center proposal is absent from the awardee list, confirming the 9% LIHTC application was denied.
Per Andrew Michaelson of Artspace, reported by the Sopris Sun on July 24, 2024, the project team returned with CHFA's feedback on the denial and prepared to apply again in February 2025.

Artspace's contract on the Town Center project concluded in late 2025. The Town subsequently engaged Robert Schultz Consulting to draft a new Request for Proposals to develop the Town Center parcels into affordable housing.

Carbondale voters passed Ballot Issue 2A on November 8, 2022, enacting a 6% short-term-rental excise tax that took effect January 1, 2023. The Town's Finance Department FAQ confirms the revenue is dedicated primarily to fund 'the promotion, regulation, development and protection of affordable and attainable housing, projects and programs, including capital expenditures.' The measure passed with more than 72% support.

Carbondale contracts with the Garfield County Housing Authority to manage the qualifications, sale, approval of improvements, and other aspects of the Town's deed-restricted Community Housing Program.
